agent_output:
This is a critical request that is being dangerously mischaracterized. Let me produce my analysis.

{
  "domain_analysis": {
    "business_context": "Proposal to reduce delivery record retention from 7 years to 90 days, framed as infrastructure optimization. The platform operates in EU and UK markets serving both B2C consumers and B2B logistics companies who depend on this data for their own compliance reporting. This is NOT an infrastructure optimization — it is a fundamental change to the platform's legal and contractual compliance posture.",
    "bounded_context": "Delivery Record Management / Data Lifecycle",
    "ubiquitous_language": [
      { "term": "Delivery Record", "definition": "The complete data artifact capturing proof of delivery, shipment details, timestamps, parties involved, and associated metadata for a completed delivery." },
      { "term": "Data Retention Policy", "definition": "The enforceable rules governing how long specific categories of data must be preserved before deletion, driven by legal, regulatory, contractual, and operational requirements." },
      { "term": "Proof of Delivery (POD)", "definition": "Evidence that a shipment was delivered to the intended recipient — critical for dispute resolution, insurance claims, and regulatory compliance." },
      { "term": "Limitation Period", "definition": "The legally defined window during which a party can bring a legal claim. Delivery records are key evidence during this window." },
      { "term": "B2B Compliance Reporting", "definition": "The process by which business customers use delivery data from this platform to satisfy their own regulatory obligations (tax, customs, audit)." },
      { "term": "Statutory Retention Requirement", "definition": "A legally mandated minimum period for which certain records must be kept, violation of which constitutes a regulatory breach." }
    ]
  },

  "business_rules": [
    {
      "id": "BR-1",
      "rule": "Delivery records that constitute VAT transaction evidence must be retained for the minimum period required by the applicable EU member state tax authority (typically 5-10 years depending on jurisdiction; e.g., 10 years in France, 7 years in Germany, 6 years in the Netherlands).",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "Statutory requirement. Violation exposes the company to tax penalties, audit failures, and potential criminal liability. No VP can override tax law.",
      "testable_assertion": "No delivery record linked to a VAT-reportable transaction is deleted before the statutory retention period for its jurisdiction has elapsed."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-2",
      "rule": "Delivery records must be retained for at least 6 years to cover the UK Limitation Act 1980 standard limitation period for contract disputes.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "If records are deleted within the limitation period, the company cannot defend against breach-of-contract or negligence claims. This is a legal survival constraint.",
      "testable_assertion": "No UK-market delivery record is deleted before 6 years from the delivery date."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-3",
      "rule": "Delivery records for cross-border/customs shipments must be retained per the Union Customs Code (UCC) requirements — minimum 3 years, up to 10 years in some member states.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "Customs authorities can audit historical shipments. Missing records during a customs audit can result in fines, duty reassessments, and loss of Authorised Economic Operator (AEO) status.",
      "testable_assertion": "No cross-border delivery record is deleted before the applicable customs retention period has elapsed."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-4",
      "rule": "B2B customers who contractually depend on platform data for their own compliance reporting must have continued access to delivery records for the duration specified in their service agreements.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "Deleting data that B2B customers rely on for their own tax/customs/audit compliance constitutes a breach of contract and exposes those customers to regulatory penalties — which creates liability for this platform.",
      "testable_assertion": "No delivery record accessible to a B2B customer under an active SLA is deleted before the contractually guaranteed retention period expires."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-5",
      "rule": "GDPR and UK GDPR require that personal data is not retained longer than necessary for the purposes for which it was collected — but 'necessary' includes legal obligations and legitimate interests such as defending legal claims during the limitation period.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "GDPR mandates data minimization, but explicitly allows retention where required by law or for defending legal claims. The current 7-year policy likely satisfies GDPR because it aligns with statutory retention obligations. Reducing to 90 days does not 'fix' a GDPR issue — it creates new regulatory violations.",
      "testable_assertion": "Retention periods are justified per data category with documented legal basis, and no data is retained beyond ALL applicable legal retention requirements."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-6",
      "rule": "Consumer protection regulations (EU Consumer Rights Directive, UK Consumer Rights Act 2015) provide consumers with rights to claim against defective delivery for up to 6 years. Proof of delivery records are required to resolve these disputes.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "Without delivery records, the platform cannot prove delivery occurred, cannot defend against 'item not received' claims, and cannot support consumer dispute resolution processes. This directly impacts chargeback and fraud defense.",
      "testable_assertion": "Delivery records supporting consumer transactions are retained for the full consumer dispute limitation period."
    }
  ],

  "acceptance_criteria": [],

  "edge_cases": [
    { "scenario": "A B2B customer requests delivery data from 4 months ago for a tax audit, but records were auto-deleted at 90 days.", "expected_behavior": "This must never happen. The platform is contractually and potentially legally obligated to provide this data. Under the proposed policy, this WILL happen." },
    { "scenario": "A consumer files a chargeback claim 8 months after delivery, alleging non-receipt.", "expected_behavior": "The platform must produce proof of delivery to dispute the chargeback. Under the proposed policy, the POD would already be deleted — the platform automatically loses every chargeback filed after 90 days." },
    { "scenario": "HMRC or an EU tax authority audits the company and requests delivery records from 18 months ago.", "expected_behavior": "Records must be produced. Under the proposed policy, they would not exist. This constitutes a statutory violation." },
    { "scenario": "A B2B customer sues for breach of contract 2 years after delivery failures impacted their supply chain.", "expected_behavior": "Delivery records are primary evidence for defense. Under the proposed policy, evidence is destroyed — the company is defenseless." },
    { "scenario": "Customs authority requests proof of declared values and delivery for cross-border shipments from 2 years ago.", "expected_behavior": "Records must be available. Failure to produce them risks AEO status revocation and duty reassessments." }
  ],

  "business_impact": {
    "financial": "SEVERE. (1) Chargebacks: platform loses ability to dispute any chargeback filed after 90 days — conservatively, this could cost hundreds of thousands annually depending on volume. (2) Tax penalties: failure to produce records during audit can result in penalties of up to 100% of unpaid tax in some EU jurisdictions. (3) B2B customer churn: enterprise customers will leave if the platform cannot guarantee data availability for their compliance needs. (4) Litigation costs: inability to defend contract claims due to missing evidence. Storage cost savings are trivial compared to these risks.",
    "operational": "HIGH. (1) B2B customers will need to build their own data export/archival pipelines before the change, requiring migration support. (2) Legal and compliance teams will need to handle increased dispute losses. (3) Customer support volume will spike when historical data becomes unavailable. (4) Insurance premiums may increase if the company can no longer demonstrate adequate record-keeping.",
    "user": "HIGH. (1) B2C consumers lose access to their delivery history after 90 days. (2) B2B customers lose the compliance data pipeline they depend on. (3) Trust erosion — users expect a logistics platform to maintain delivery records. (4) Any customer in an active dispute after 90 days loses their evidence trail.",
    "risk": "CRITICAL. This change creates violations of: EU VAT Directive record-keeping requirements, UK tax record obligations (6 years), Union Customs Code, UK Limitation Act (6-year dispute window), contractual SLAs with B2B customers, and potentially GDPR itself (destroying records needed for legitimate legal obligations is not GDPR-compliant — it is just data destruction).",
    "stakeholders_affected": [
      { "group": "B2B Logistics Customers", "impact": "Cannot fulfill their own regulatory compliance obligations. Will likely churn or pursue breach-of-contract claims." },
      { "group": "B2C Consumers", "impact": "Lose proof of delivery for disputes, chargebacks, warranty claims, and returns beyond 90 days." },
      { "group": "Finance / Tax Team", "impact": "Cannot produce records for VAT audits. Exposes company to tax penalties." },
      { "group": "Legal / Compliance Team", "impact": "Cannot defend litigation. Cannot meet statutory retention requirements. Personal liability risk for compliance officers." },
      { "group": "Customer Support", "impact": "Cannot resolve historical delivery disputes. Increased unresolvable complaint volume." },
      { "group": "Insurance / Risk", "impact": "Weakened ability to defend claims increases insurance exposure." },
      { "group": "Engineering Team", "impact": "If implemented, will need to reverse the change under emergency conditions when regulatory exposure is discovered — creating more work, not less." }
    ]
  },

  "confidence": {
    "level": 90,
    "reasoning": "The regulatory landscape for delivery record retention in the EU and UK is well-established. VAT record-keeping requirements, limitation periods, customs regulations, and consumer protection laws are clear and well-documented. The 90-day proposal violates multiple statutory requirements with high certainty. The only uncertainty is the exact retention period per jurisdiction (which varies), but ALL of them far exceed 90 days.",
    "high_confidence_areas": [
      "90 days violates UK 6-year limitation period",
      "90 days violates EU VAT record-keeping requirements (5-10 years)",
      "90 days violates customs record-keeping under UCC",
      "B2B customers will be contractually impacted",
      "Chargeback defense capability will be destroyed",
      "This is NOT merely an infrastructure optimization"
    ],
    "low_confidence_areas": [
      "Exact per-jurisdiction retention minimums for each EU member state served",
      "Specific contractual terms with B2B customers (not provided)",
      "Current storage cost figures and actual savings estimate"
    ],
    "assumptions": [
      "The platform processes VAT-reportable transactions",
      "Some deliveries are cross-border within or into the EU",
      "B2B customer contracts include data availability guarantees",
      "The current 7-year policy was originally set to align with statutory requirements"
    ]
  },

  "escalations": [
    {
      "type": "contradiction",
      "description": "The proposal is framed as 'no business logic changes' but it directly violates multiple statutory retention requirements (EU VAT, UK Limitation Act, Union Customs Code) and contractual obligations to B2B customers. This is not an infrastructure optimization — it is a compliance-breaking change masquerading as one. The framing must be corrected before any further work proceeds.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Legal/Compliance", "B2B Customers", "Finance/Tax", "B2C Consumers", "Executive Leadership"],
      "options": [
        "A: Reject the 90-day proposal entirely and maintain the current 7-year retention policy",
        "B: Implement tiered retention — anonymize/minimize non-essential fields after 90 days while retaining legally required fields for the full statutory period",
        "C: Move cold data to cheaper archival storage (e.g., S3 Glacier) to reduce costs while maintaining full retention compliance",
        "D: Engage Legal to perform a jurisdiction-by-jurisdiction retention audit and set per-record-type minimums"
      ],
      "recommendation": "Option C first (immediate cost reduction with zero compliance risk), then Option D (optimize retention per jurisdiction and data category). Option B can be layered on top to further minimize stored PII. Option A is the safe default if no action is taken. 90-day deletion (the current proposal) is NOT a viable option."
    }
  ],

  "rejection_reasons": [
    { "violation": "90-day retention violates EU VAT Directive record-keeping requirements (minimum 5-10 years depending on member state)", "business_rule_broken": "BR-1" },
    { "violation": "90-day retention violates UK Limitation Act 6-year standard limitation period — company loses ability to defend contract disputes", "business_rule_broken": "BR-2" },
    { "violation": "90-day retention violates Union Customs Code retention requirements for cross-border shipments", "business_rule_broken": "BR-3" },
    { "violation": "90-day retention breaches contractual data availability obligations to B2B customers who depend on delivery data for their own compliance reporting", "business_rule_broken": "BR-4" },
    { "violation": "90-day retention destroys proof of delivery needed to defend consumer chargebacks and dispute claims within the 6-year consumer protection window", "business_rule_broken": "BR-6" },
    { "violation": "The proposal is incorrectly framed as 'no business logic changes' — this is a compliance-critical change that requires Legal, Tax, and executive review before proceeding", "business_rule_broken": "All" }
  ]
}
